synthesis_text: COVID-19 differs from the common cold and flu in several significant ways. Firstly, COVID-19 can lead to serious complications and death, whereas the common cold and flu are generally milder illnesses [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00004]. Additionally, COVID-19 symptoms can include loss of taste or smell, which is not typical for the common cold or flu [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00002]. The virus responsible for COVID-19 is SARS-CoV-2, distinct from the viruses that cause the common cold and flu [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]. Furthermore, COVID-19 can cause long-term symptoms, known as post-COVID-19 syndrome, which is not commonly associated with the common cold or flu [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00017]. Lastly, COVID-19 spreads primarily through respiratory droplets and can also be transmitted by asymptomatic individuals, similar to the flu [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00008].

COVID-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, while the common cold and flu are caused by different viruses [node_c:0:b45f35f8]. Symptoms of COVID-19 can be similar to those of the flu and common cold, requiring tests for confirmation [node_c:1:bdffaeed]. However, COVID-19 symptoms can range from mild to severe, with some individuals being asymptomatic [node_c:2:368cb81b]. Unlike the common cold or flu, COVID-19 can lead to serious complications such as multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children [node_c:3:77fb6eda] and long-term effects known as Long COVID [node_c:4:c30a5752]. Additionally, COVID-19 can cause symptoms like loss of taste or smell, which are not typical for the common cold or flu [node_b:1:43969c1c].
